briefdescription.para: Creates a default UiColor (transparent black, no ID).

memberdef.argsstring: (float r, float g, float b, float a=1.0f)
memberdef.name: UiColor

briefdescription.para: Creates a UiColor with explicit RGBA components.
Creates a UiColor from a Vector4.
Creates a UiColor with a string identifier.

detaileddescription.para: The actual RGBA value is resolved from the current theme when Resolve() is called.

briefdescription.para: Creates a UiColor with a string identifier (rvalue overload).

detaileddescription.para: The actual RGBA value is resolved from the current theme when Resolve() is called.
This overload enables moving the string into the internal token registry to avoid an extra copy when the caller provides an rvalue.

briefdescription.para: Creates a UiColor from a hex RGB value.
Returns whether this UiColor holds a string color ID.

simplesect.para: True if this UiColor was constructed with a color ID
The color ID, or an empty string if this is a direct RGBA color

briefdescription.para: Resolves and returns the RGBA value as a Vector4.

detaileddescription.para: If this UiColor has a color ID, the value is looked up from the current theme. If the ID is not found, returns the fallback mColor value (default transparent black). If this UiColor has direct RGBA values, returns them as-is.

briefdescription.para: Returns a UiColor with alpha scaled by the given factor.

listitem.para: Token: the resolved alpha is scaled by factor

briefdescription.para: Returns a UiColor with alpha replaced by the given value.

listitem.para: Token: the resolved alpha is replaced with alpha

briefdescription.para: A color value that can hold either direct RGBA components or a string identifier that is resolved at runtime via the current theme.

detaileddescription.para: When constructed with RGBA values, UiColor behaves like a lightweight wrapper around Vector4. When constructed with a string color ID, the actual RGBA values are looked up from the current ThemeLoaderInterface at the point of Resolve().
